title: AMC Q1 Earnings Preview: Here's What You'll Want to Know
text: AMC Entertainment Holdings faced severe challenges during the pandemic, with nearly all 1,000 theaters temporarily closing. By the earnings report date (May 6, 2021), approximately 98% had reopened, though many operated with reduced capacity. The company was losing an estimated $130 million per month during shutdowns. Analysts projected Q1 revenue of $153 million—representing an 84% decrease year-over-year. The article noted that moviegoers remained hesitant to return despite reopened theaters. Two factors contributed: studios delayed major film releases and released many titles simultaneously on streaming platforms like Disney+, reducing the urgency for theatrical viewing. The article noted that AMC stock had surged 342% year-to-date primarily due to a short-squeeze trading frenzy. The author cautiously suggested that prospective investors wait for the price of AMC to pull back substantially before starting a position.
